items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights` **Service directory:** `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/` **Triggered by:** PR #58443 (commit e2671314d) by `@ArthurMa1978` ## What Changed PR #58443 promoted `Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights` to its **first stable release (1.0.0)** as part of a batch ARM SDK refresh. Version `1.0.0` was stamped on 2026-04-21. ## Gaps Found - **Key concepts section is empty/generic** — only links to the generic Azure SDK introduction page. It should describe CustomerInsights-specific concepts: `HubResource`, Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, Relationships, Views, KPIs, and Authorization Policies. - **No inline code examples** — the "Examples" section only points to the generic `aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` link without any service-specific snippets. At minimum, creating a Hub and listing its profiles should be demonstrated. - **No Samples test files** — the `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subfolder with snippet-annotated NUnit test methods. These are required to generate README snippets via `Update-Snippets.ps1`. - **README header and "Getting Started" still use beta-era generic boilerplate** — for a GA release the description should be updated to drop references to "Public Preview" and use definitive language about capabilities.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### Edit `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md` Replace the `## Key concepts` section (currently a single generic link) with a service-specific section: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### Hub A **Hub** (`HubResource`) is the top-level Customer Insights resource that lives in an Azure Resource Group. All other Customer Insights resources (Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, etc.) are scoped under a Hub. ### Profiles and Interactions **Profiles** represent the customer entities (e.g., a person or account). **Interactions** represent customer activities (e.g., a purchase or a support call). Both are defined as schema definitions inside a Hub. ### Connectors and Connector Mappings **Connectors** define the data source (e.g., Azure Blob Storage) used to ingest data. **Connector Mappings** describe how incoming data maps to Profiles or Interactions. ### Views, KPIs, and Relationships These sub-resources let you surface aggregated metrics, define data relationships across Profiles, and create custom views over the data. ``` Replace the `## Examples` section with a placeholder that will be filled in by the snippet tool: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Hub ```C# Snippet:CreateHub ``` ### List Hubs in a subscription ```C# Snippet:ListHubs ``` ``` #### Create `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` Create the file with the following content: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights; using Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample compiles")] public async Task CreateHub() { #region Snippet:CreateHub ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(""); HubCollection hubs = resourceGroup.GetHubs(); HubData hubData = new HubData(AzureLocation.WestUS); ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "", hubData); HubResource hub = lro.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Hub created: {hub.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample compiles")] public async Task ListHubs() { #region Snippet:ListHubs ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(""); await foreach (HubResource hub in resourceGroup.GetHubs()) { Console.WriteLine($"Hub: {hub.Data.Name}, Location: {hub.Data.Location}"); } #endregion } } } ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippets are defined in the test file above using `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. **Snippet 1: `CreateHub`** - Test file: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` — method `CreateHub` - README location: under the `### Create a Hub` heading in `## Examples` - Placeholder: `` ```C# Snippet:CreateHub `` followed by closing ` ``` ` **Snippet 2: `ListHubs`** - Test file: `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` — method `ListHubs` - README location: under the `### List Hubs in a subscription` heading in `## Examples` - Placeholder: `` ```C# Snippet:ListHubs `` followed by closing ` ``` ` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README must contain these sections in this order. Current status: | Section | Status | |---------|--------| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Too generic — needs CustomerInsights-specific content (see Step 1) | | Examples | ❌ Missing inline code — only a generic link (see Step 1 + Step 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 customer-insights` — injects `#region Snippet:` code into README.md placeholder blocks. Check `git diff` afterwards to confirm README was updated. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 customer-insights` — regenerates public API listing files under `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/api/` (only needed if public types changed). 5. `dotnet format sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/` — ensures consistent code formatting.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [CustomerInsights] Docs: Update README for first stable release of Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights
